fields.definition_origin: The original 'Six Onboarding Antipatterns' framework derives from the PDF 'Strategic Architecture and Psychological Frameworks for Mobile Application Onboarding' (.agent/Deep Dive Into App Onboarding.pdf, 2025), which synthesizes Nielsen Norman Group's UX research on mobile onboarding (Raluca Budiu, Page Laubheimer, Aurora Harley, multiple 2018-2024 articles), Apple HIG's onboarding chapter, Google's Android UX Design guides, and over a decade of mobile-onboarding teardowns from Mobbin, Growth.Design, Reforge, and Appcues. The six canonical antipatterns: (1) Wall-of-Text + Cognitive Overload, (2) Premature Authentication, (3) No-Escape Room, (4) Contextless Permissions, (5) Wasted Loading Screens, (6) Ignored Error States. The 2024-2026 modern extensions emerged from regulatory pressure (EU DFA draft Q4 2026, FTC Click-to-Cancel 2025-07-14, Apple Guideline 3.1.2 toggle-paywall ban 2026-01) and AI-codegen-era observation: (7) Decision Paralysis (5+ plans), (8) Generic Celebration (mismatched archetype voice), (9) Toggle Paywall (Apple-banned 2026-01), (10) Confirm-Shaming (BEUC SHEIN complaint 2025).
fields.core_mechanism: Each antipattern violates a specific psychological or architectural principle that compounds with others. (1) WALL-OF-TEXT — violates cognitive load theory (Sweller 1988); deck-of-carousel introduction taxes working memory (3-7 item limit, Cowan 2001) and front-loads value-explanation before value-experience. (2) PREMATURE AUTH — sign-up wall before any value demo violates the 'Aha-before-friction' principle (Sean Ellis, ProductLed); user has no reason to invest authentication effort. (3) NO-ESCAPE ROOM — rigid 12-step unskippable tour violates user agency (Self-Determination Theory autonomy need, Deci & Ryan); creates anxious-rather-than-engaged user. (4) CONTEXTLESS PERMISSIONS — cold native dialog without priming tanks opt-in (iOS ATT 25% baseline vs 60-80% with priming); violates explanation-before-ask principle. (5) WASTED LOADING — blank spinner during fetch wastes engineered-suspense opportunity; trust decays during dead time. (6) IGNORED ERROR STATES — broken state on network failure abandons user mid-flow without recovery affordance. The four modern extensions: (7) DECISION PARALYSIS — Hick's Law (1952) + Iyengar-Lepper jam-paradox (2000) — 5+ plans tank conversion. (8) GENERIC CELEBRATION — archetype mismatch (Magician brand with Jester confetti, item 52) breaks brand coherence. (9) TOGGLE PAYWALL — Apple Guideline 3.1.2 explicit ban (effective 2026-01) — predatory subscription disclosure. (10) CONFIRM-SHAMING — 'No, I don't want to save money' decline-language flagged by BEUC June 2025 SHEIN complaint and EU DFA draft as manipulative.
fields.neuroscience_correlate: Working memory ceiling (3-7 items, Cowan 2001) underlies Wall-of-Text. Action-prediction-error (dopaminergic VTA) signals broken expectations in Wasted Loading and Ignored Errors. Anterior cingulate cortex (ACC) conflict monitoring spikes during confusion (Ignored Error States). Decision-fatigue (Baumeister et al. ego-depletion, contested) operationalizes Decision Paralysis. Confirm-shaming triggers reactance (Brehm 1966) — user feels manipulated, distrust spikes.
fields.empirical_evidence: (1) NN/g reports (multiple 2018-2024) document each antipattern with usability-test failure data — Wall-of-Text reduces task-completion 30-50%. (2) Apple HIG, Google Android UX guides explicitly prescribe avoiding Premature Auth and Contextless Permissions. (3) Adapty 2026 onboarding teardowns: bottom-decile flows exhibit 4+ antipatterns; top-decile zero. (4) Apple's own Guideline 3.1.2 ban on toggle paywalls (announced August 2024, effective January 2026) backed by App Store review data showing user complaints. (5) BEUC SHEIN complaint (June 2025) cited confirm-shaming and obstructive-cancellation as DFA dark patterns. (6) Disney $10M FTC settlement (December 2025) — children's onboarding antipatterns. (7) iOS push opt-in: 25% cold-dialog baseline vs 60-80% with priming (multiple industry studies, OneSignal benchmarks 2024). (8) Iyengar & Lepper (2000) 'When Choice is Demotivating' — jam-shelf experiment, 30% vs 3% purchase rate (24 vs 6 jam options). (9) Sweller cognitive-load original papers (1988) for Wall-of-Text. (10) Mobbin/Growth.Design teardowns 2023-2026 catalogue antipattern instances per app. (11) AI-codegen observation: published case studies of v0 / Lovable / Bolt / Cursor outputs reveal recurring instantiations (rounded-xl wall-of-text intro carousels, generic Lottie celebrations, indigo-500 toggle paywalls).
fields.measurable_outcome_metric: Per-antipattern incident count in audit (target: zero), step-level abandonment (Wall-of-Text spikes early-step drop-off), permission opt-in rate (Contextless Permissions tanks), trial-conversion rate (Premature Auth tanks), customer-complaint volume (Confirm-Shaming, Toggle Paywall), App Store / Play Store rejection count.
fields.evidence_strength: industry_benchmark + practitioner_opinion + legal_requirement (Toggle Paywall is legal_requirement post-2026-01)
fields.conversion_lift_benchmark: Adapty 2026: top-decile vs bottom-decile (zero-antipattern vs many-antipattern) flows show 2-3x paywall reach gap and 3-5x trial conversion gap. Specific per-antipattern lift: priming permissions +40-60% opt-in; removing toggle paywall (post-ban) preserves App Store availability.
fields.engineering_tactics:
AUDIT EVERY ONBOARDING SCREEN AGAINST THE 10-POINT CHECKLIST. For each antipattern below, document: violation, AI-codegen-slop instantiation, counter-prompt, fix.

(1) WALL-OF-TEXT + COGNITIVE OVERLOAD
- Violation: Multi-paragraph value-prop text on welcome screen, deck-of-carousel introduction.
- AI-CODEGEN SLOP VERSION: AI generates `<View><Text style={{fontSize: 16}}>{LONG_PARAGRAPH_DESCRIBING_FEATURES}</Text></View>` with bulleted feature list, three-card carousel with '🚀 Fast' / '✨ Smart' / '🔒 Secure' captions.
- COUNTER-PROMPT: 'No screen may have more than 12 words of body copy. Forbid carousel introductions. Forbid emoji-bulleted feature lists. Replace with single full-bleed image + 6-word headline + single CTA. If feature explanation needed, defer to contextual coach mark (item 22).'
- Fix: Single-question-per-screen conversational pattern (item 28); progressive disclosure (item 16).

(2) PREMATURE AUTHENTICATION
- Violation: Sign-up wall on screen 1; require account before showing core feature.
- AI-CODEGEN SLOP VERSION: AI generates `<EmailInput /> <PasswordInput /> <CreateAccountButton />` as the first screen, with no value demo.
- COUNTER-PROMPT: 'Forbid email/password collection before any value demo. Show core feature interactively for at least 3 screens. Defer auth to after value-felt moment OR until persistence required (item 60 magic-link / item 61 biometric pattern). Defer permissions and auth to after AI plan reveal (item 12).'
- Fix: Magic-link / passkey / Apple/Google sign-in deferred until item 24 permission ladder reaches the auth rung. Anonymous_id pre-auth state (item 33).

(3) NO-ESCAPE ROOM
- Violation: Unskippable 12-step tour with no Back button or Skip option.
- AI-CODEGEN SLOP VERSION: AI generates `<Stack screenOptions={{gestureEnabled: false, headerLeft: null}}>` with no skip affordance.
- COUNTER-PROMPT: 'Every onboarding screen must support back-navigation (gestureEnabled: true, hardware back button on Android). Provide "Skip" affordance on at least 30% of screens. Skip routes to default-state onboarding completion. Forbid: blocking back navigation, hiding system gestures.'
- Fix: FSM (item 30) BACK event with isFirstStep guard; Skip transitions to soft paywall or completed.

(4) CONTEXTLESS PERMISSIONS
- Violation: Cold native push/ATT/camera/location dialog without priming.
- AI-CODEGEN SLOP VERSION: AI calls `messaging().requestPermission()` on screen 1 mount with no UI explanation. Or `Tracking.requestTrackingPermissionsAsync()` immediately on app open.
- COUNTER-PROMPT: 'Every OS permission must be preceded by a soft-ask priming screen (item 65). Order: priming screen → soft-ask CTA → native dialog. Only request permission tied to an immediate user-felt benefit (push: "to remind you of your daily streak"; camera: "to track your meal"; location: "to find classes near you"). Forbid: cold native dialog on app launch.'
- Fix: Item 24 permission ladder + item 65 pre-permission soft-ask screen.

(5) WASTED LOADING SCREENS
- Violation: Blank ActivityIndicator during onboarding fetch / AI plan generation.
- AI-CODEGEN SLOP VERSION: AI generates `{loading ? <ActivityIndicator /> : <Content />}` with no narrative copy or theatrical pacing.
- COUNTER-PROMPT: 'Forbid blank spinner during onboarding loading. Use theatrical loader (item 13) with narrative copy stack ("Analyzing your responses → Cross-referencing 2.4M users like you → Building your plan") for 4-7 seconds. Reanimated 3 morphing copy transitions. Show progress hint, never indeterminate. Pair with progress bar (item 29) reaching 100% at the loader.'
- Fix: Item 13 personalized-plan loader; item 29 progress indicator never indeterminate.

(6) IGNORED ERROR STATES
- Violation: White screen / crash / silent failure on network error.
- AI-CODEGEN SLOP VERSION: AI generates `try { await fetch(...) } catch {}` empty catch; no UI error state; no retry CTA.
- COUNTER-PROMPT: 'Every async operation must have a designed error state with retry CTA, fallback content, and human copy ("Trouble connecting — let\'s try again in a moment"). Forbid: empty catch blocks, white-screen crashes, console.error-only error handling. Persist state via item 32 so resumed sessions see no data loss.'
- Fix: FSM error states (planError, syncError) with explicit retry/skip transitions; offline fallback content cached.

(7) DECISION PARALYSIS (5+ PLANS)  [MODERN EXTENSION]
- Violation: 5+ subscription plans presented without recommended highlight; user freezes.
- AI-CODEGEN SLOP VERSION: AI generates pricing page with 5 columns: Free / Basic / Pro / Premium / Enterprise — equal weight, no default selection.
- COUNTER-PROMPT: '3-4 plans MAX (Hick\'s Law). Pre-select "Most Popular" plan with badge. Default to annual or weekly+trial (Adapty 2026 winning SKU). Forbid: 5+ plan tiers, equal-weight presentation, no default highlight.'
- Fix: Item 38 plan selection psychology — 3 plans with decoy effect, default highlight on target.

(8) GENERIC CELEBRATION (MISMATCHED ARCHETYPE VOICE)  [MODERN EXTENSION]
- Violation: Stock confetti + 'Success!' toast on Sage/Caregiver/Outlaw archetype apps — flattens brand distinctiveness.
- AI-CODEGEN SLOP VERSION: AI generates `<Lottie source={require('./confetti.json')} /> <Text>Success! 🎉</Text>` regardless of brand archetype.
- COUNTER-PROMPT: 'Celebration must match brand archetype (item 52). Magician: subtle iridescent particle dissolve. Caregiver: warm-color soft scale-in with reassuring copy. Sage: minimal acknowledgment, no flourish. Hero: bold ascending burst. Outlaw: jagged, anti-establishment glitch. Forbid: stock Lottie confetti unless archetype is Jester. Forbid: "Success! 🎉" generic copy.'
- Fix: Archetype-conditioned celebration component library; item 52 voice/visual table.

(9) TOGGLE PAYWALL  [BANNED 2026-01]
- Violation: Single-toggle plan picker ("Free trial: ON / OFF") obscuring subscription terms — Apple Guideline 3.1.2 explicit ban effective 2026-01.
- AI-CODEGEN SLOP VERSION: AI generates `<Switch value={isTrialOn} onValueChange={setIsTrialOn} />` paywall pattern.
- COUNTER-PROMPT: 'Forbid toggle-paywall pattern entirely. Use side-by-side plan selection (two cards: weekly+trial vs annual) OR timeline-transparency layout (visual timeline showing day 1 free, day 7 charged). Apple App Store rejection guarantee post-2026-01 if toggle present.'
- Fix: Item 38 plan selection — side-by-side or timeline; item 34 hard paywall climax.

(10) CONFIRM-SHAMING  [DFA Q4 2026 RISK]
- Violation: Decline button copy reads 'No, I don\'t want to save money' / 'No thanks, I prefer paying full price' — manipulative reactance trigger.
- AI-CODEGEN SLOP VERSION: AI generates `<Button title="No, I don\'t want to lose 10lb" onPress={dismiss} />` style decline copy.
- COUNTER-PROMPT: 'Decline buttons must use neutral language: "Maybe later" / "Not now" / "Skip". Forbid emotionally-manipulative decline copy ("No, I don\'t want to..."). BEUC + EU DFA draft Q4 2026 cite confirm-shaming as dark pattern.'
- Fix: Decline copy review; standard neutral microcopy library.

AUDIT IMPLEMENTATION: build a CI lint that scans onboarding files for: emoji-bulleted feature lists, ActivityIndicator without context, Switch in paywall components, regex matches on confirm-shaming phrases, plan-count >4. Fail PR if violations found.
fields.real_world_examples: (1) Pre-2022 Mint/Intuit budget app: Premature Auth (sign-up screen 1) — redesigned 2024 to value-first. (2) Older Snapchat (2013) onboarding: Contextless Permissions (camera + contacts cold-asked); modern Snap primes both. (3) Many 2024-2026 AI-wrapper apps from Lovable/Bolt: indigo-500 Wall-of-Text intro carousels. (4) Lifesum (older versions): Wasted Loading + Ignored Error States, redesigned 2024. (5) Apple-rejected apps post-Aug-2024: toggle-paywall instances (multiple developer reports). (6) BEUC SHEIN complaint June 2025 cites Confirm-Shaming + obstructive cancellation. (7) Disney $10M FTC settlement Dec 2025 — children's app onboarding antipatterns. (8) Counter-positive examples: Cal AI (zero antipatterns; canonical reference), Headspace 2024, Mau Baron Prayer Lock, Headway, Calm — all audit clean. (9) Counter-positive: BlueSky onboarding (open source) — back navigation, magic-link auth, contextual permissions. (10) Adapty 2026 case-study apps before/after antipattern audit: typical 30-60% paywall-reach lift after fixes.
fields.mobile_vs_web_diffs: Antipatterns largely apply equally; mobile-specific severities: Contextless Permissions higher impact (OS dialog interruption costlier on mobile), Wasted Loading higher impact (mobile context-switching costs trust faster), No-Escape Room more egregious (system back button violation feels broken). Web equivalents: similar logic, different UI (form-style auth wall, browser back). Confirm-Shaming and Toggle Paywall apply to both.
fields.platform_design_language_alignment: Antipatterns FIGHT against both Liquid Glass (which prizes restraint, depth, photographic clarity — Wall-of-Text breaks the elegance) and Material 3 Expressive (which prizes meaningful celebration moments — Generic Celebration ignores the system's expressive intent). Following platform languages naturally avoids many antipatterns.
fields.category_fit: Apply to ALL onboarding categories. Strictest enforcement in: regulated categories (fintech, healthcare, kids' apps) where Toggle Paywall and Premature Auth carry compliance risk. Magnitude varies — wellness/AI-wrapper antipattern severity 2-3x worse for completion than utility apps.
fields.solo_dev_feasibility: same_day for audit; 1_week for fixes if multiple antipatterns present. Audit is a 1-2 hour walkthrough; fixes scale with fix complexity (Toggle Paywall replacement = 1-2 days; Permission Priming = 1-2 days; archetype-conditioned celebrations = 1 week).
fields.tooling_options: AUDIT: Mobbin (browse competitor flows), Growth.Design teardowns, NN/g articles, Apple HIG audit checklist. AUTOMATED: ESLint custom rules detecting antipattern code shapes (carousel intro, ActivityIndicator without copy, Switch in paywall). REVIEW: Plotline / Adapty / Superwall pre-built compliant patterns. CMP for permissions: Axeptio, OneTrust, Usercentrics. PAYWALL: RevenueCat / Adapty / Superwall pre-built non-toggle layouts.
fields.ai_dependency: none. Optional: Claude/GPT-4 audit of screenshots/code for antipattern detection.
fields.claude_design_application: Instruct Claude to audit + fix. Prompt fragment 1: 'Audit this onboarding flow against the 10-Point Antipattern Checklist: (1) Wall-of-Text + Cognitive Overload, (2) Premature Auth, (3) No-Escape Room, (4) Contextless Permissions, (5) Wasted Loading, (6) Ignored Errors, (7) Decision Paralysis (5+ plans), (8) Generic Celebration (archetype-mismatched), (9) Toggle Paywall (Apple-banned 2026-01), (10) Confirm-Shaming. For each violation: cite the screen, describe the violation, propose fix, output corrected component code. Forbid: declaring "no antipatterns found" without per-screen evidence.' Prompt fragment 2: 'Generate the welcome screen for our onboarding. FORBID: deck-of-cards introduction carousel, multi-paragraph value-prop text, emoji-bulleted feature list, sign-up form on this screen, cold-call permission dialog, blank spinner, white-on-error screen, 5+ plan picker, generic confetti, Switch toggle for paywall, decline button reading "No, I don\'t want to lose weight". REQUIRE: single full-bleed image, 6-word headline, single primary CTA "Begin" advancing to the questionnaire (item 28 conversational pattern). Brand color #E36B2C, Cabinet Grotesk display 56pt, no Inter, no indigo-500.' Prompt fragment 3: 'Add CI lint rules: (a) flag <ActivityIndicator /> not wrapped in TheatricalLoader, (b) flag emoji in JSX strings within onboarding/ folder, (c) flag <Switch /> in components named *Paywall*, (d) flag arrays of 5+ plan objects, (e) flag regex matches on confirm-shaming phrases. Output the .eslintrc.js custom rules.'
fields.stitch_application: Stitch must be told what NOT to design. Prompt fragment 1: 'Mockup the onboarding welcome screen. EXPLICITLY AVOID: (1) deck-of-cards carousel introduction with 3-5 feature cards, (2) wall-of-text paragraph below the headline, (3) multiple CTAs (single primary only), (4) sign-up form fields, (5) emoji bullets, (6) trust-row of fake company logos, (7) hover-scale-105 animations, (8) indigo-500 / purple-blue gradient. EXPLICITLY DO: full-bleed background photograph (warm-toned, slight grain), 56pt headline left-aligned in Cabinet Grotesk weight 350, color #1A1816, single CTA bottom-anchored ("Begin" — neutral verb, NOT "Get Started!" with exclamation), cream surface #F4EFE6 below the photo. Forbid Inter; use Cabinet Grotesk + Söhne.' Prompt fragment 2: 'Counter-pattern catalog: design 4 frames showing the canonical antipattern instances (Wall-of-Text intro carousel, Toggle Paywall pre-2026, Generic Confetti success, Confirm-Shaming decline). Label each with the antipattern name + ban-list reference. These are negative examples for designer review.'
fields.ai_codegen_failure_mode: AI codegen instantiates ALL six original antipatterns simultaneously when given an ambiguous 'build me an onboarding flow' prompt: deck-of-cards intro carousel (Wall-of-Text), email-on-screen-1 (Premature Auth), no Skip button (No-Escape), `messaging().requestPermission()` on mount (Contextless Permissions), `<ActivityIndicator />` for AI fetch (Wasted Loading), empty catch block (Ignored Errors). Plus the modern extensions: 5-plan pricing grid (Decision Paralysis), stock Lottie confetti (Generic Celebration), `<Switch>` toggle paywall (banned), `"No, I don\'t want to save"` decline copy (Confirm-Shaming). Counter-prompt: paste the 10-Point Audit verbatim into every onboarding-generation prompt; require the AI to declare which antipatterns it actively avoided and how. Output: 'Antipattern Audit Compliance: [pass/fail table]' before generating components.
fields.anti_patterns: Self-defining — this item IS the antipattern catalog. Anti-pattern of the antipattern audit itself: declaring 'we follow best practices' without per-screen audit evidence; treating audit as one-time release-gate vs continuous practice; not propagating audit to designers (visual antipatterns: AI slop tells per item AI_Slop_Tells.json) or analytics (untracked steps, item 43).
fields.regulatory_risk: High for several antipatterns. (a) Toggle Paywall — Apple Guideline 3.1.2 ban effective 2026-01; rejection guaranteed. (b) Confirm-Shaming — EU DFA Q4 2026 draft + BEUC SHEIN complaint signal enforcement appetite. (c) Contextless Permissions for sensitive data (location, health) without justification — GDPR Art. 13 transparency violation. (d) Premature Auth that collects unnecessary PII — GDPR Art. 5(1)(c) data minimization + Apple 5.1.1(v). (e) Wall-of-Text obscuring subscription terms — FTC Click-to-Cancel disclosure requirement (effective 2025-07-14). (f) Children's apps with any antipattern — COPPA 2026 + UK AADC + California AADC enforcement (Disney $10M Dec 2025). Wall-of-Text + Premature Auth + Confirm-Shaming carry MEDIUM-HIGH legal risk; others low-to-medium.
fields.platform_constraints_2026: Apple App Store: Toggle Paywall BANNED Guideline 3.1.2 (effective 2026-01). Apple HIG explicitly discourages Premature Auth (Guideline 4.8 Sign-In with Apple alternatives required if other social offered). Apple Guideline 5.1.1(v) data minimization restricts Premature Auth's PII over-collection. Apple Guideline 4.5.1 forbids contextless permissions for sensitive APIs. Google Play Data Safety form requires permission justification (mitigates Contextless Permissions). EU DFA Q4 2026 will enforce Confirm-Shaming, Decision Paralysis, manipulative paywall variants.
fields.accessibility_implications: Several antipatterns intersect with WCAG 2.2: (a) Wall-of-Text fails 3.1.5 reading-level guidelines for cognitive accessibility. (b) No-Escape Room fails 2.1.2 No Keyboard Trap and respect-back-button. (c) Wasted Loading fails 2.2.6 timeouts (no warning of long wait). (d) Ignored Error States fails 3.3.1 Error Identification. (e) Generic Celebration with motion-only feedback fails 2.3.3 Animation from Interactions for users with prefers-reduced-motion. (f) Decision Paralysis intersects with cognitive accessibility — 5+ choices overload. Audit must include a11y checks per antipattern.
fields.pitfalls_caveats: (1) Antipattern audit becomes performative checkbox without genuine fix — do real fixes. (2) New antipatterns emerge (post-2026 likely: AI-impersonation, dark-pattern in conversational LLMs, voice-based confirm-shaming) — keep audit living. (3) Platform-specific drift — iOS audit checklist differs from Android in permission specifics. (4) Audit fatigue across teams — designate single owner. (5) Antipattern fixes can introduce new ones — fix Wall-of-Text by stripping content can create Wasted Welcome (no value-prop). (6) Zero-tolerance not always feasible — legacy app with deep coupling may need phased fix. (7) AI codegen will continue manufacturing antipatterns — audit cannot rely solely on AI; human review mandatory. (8) Toggle Paywall ban (Apple) doesn't apply to web checkout (item 40 web parallel funnel) — but BEUC + DFA may pursue cross-platform.
fields.cross_cultural_validity: Most antipatterns cross-culturally valid. Cultural variance: Confirm-Shaming severity varies — direct decline language acceptable in DE/NL, more aggressive perception in JP/KR (face-saving cultures); soften copy further in those markets. Wall-of-Text more tolerated in CN (information-dense web design conventions), still negative on mobile. Decision Paralysis more severe in cultures with low decision-making autonomy norms. Generic Celebration: Western Jester confetti reads embarrassing in JP/KR onboarding — softer celebration register required. RTL: No-Escape and Contextless Permissions equally bad; Wall-of-Text reading order requires verification.
fields.prescribed_action: Run the 10-point Antipattern Audit on every onboarding flow before launch. Document each violation with screen, AI-codegen-slop instantiation, counter-prompt, and fix. Re-audit on every onboarding change. Ban: Toggle Paywall (post-2026-01 Apple), Confirm-Shaming, Wall-of-Text intro carousels, Premature Auth, Cold Permission Dialogs, Blank Loading Spinners, Empty Error Handlers, 5+ Plan pickers, archetype-mismatched Generic Celebrations. CI enforcement: ESLint rules for code-shape antipatterns. Cross-reference with Visual AI-Slop audit (item AI_Slop_Tells.json).
fields.questionnaire_relevance: Indirect — questionnaire (items 26, 27, 28) properly designed naturally avoids Wall-of-Text (single question per screen), No-Escape (Skip + Back), Wasted Loading (theatrical loader, item 13). Toggle Paywall and Confirm-Shaming apply to paywall handoff post-questionnaire.
fields.aha_relevance: Indirect — Wasted Loading at the AI plan reveal moment specifically destroys Aha. Engineered theatrical loader (item 13) is the canonical fix.
fields.measurement_horizon: Day_0 (operational; pre-launch audit gate) + Day_30 (post-fix conversion lift)
fields.emotional_archetype_alignment: Antipatterns universally bad regardless of archetype, but specific violations more devastating per archetype: Caregiver brand + Confirm-Shaming = catastrophic mismatch; Sage brand + Wall-of-Text = ironic violation (Sage should respect cognitive load); Magician + Wasted Loading = wasted theatrical opportunity. Item 52 alignment fix per archetype.
fields.anti_pattern_paired: This IS the master antipattern catalog. Cross-references: item 16 (Progressive Disclosure) defeats Wall-of-Text; item 60 (Magic Link) defeats Premature Auth; item 30 (FSM with BACK guards) defeats No-Escape; item 24 (Permission Priming) + item 65 (Pre-Permission Soft-Ask) defeat Contextless Permissions; item 13 (Theatrical Loader) defeats Wasted Loading; item 32 (Persistence) + FSM error states defeat Ignored Errors; item 38 (Plan Selection Psychology) defeats Decision Paralysis; item 52 (Archetype Voice) defeats Generic Celebration; item 38 alternatives defeat Toggle Paywall; neutral microcopy defeats Confirm-Shaming.
sources: [Goodwin — DFA From Dark Patterns to Fair Play (Nov 2025)](https://www.goodwinlaw.com/en/insights/publications/2025/11/alerts-practices-antc-from-dark-patterns-to-fair-play)
uncertain: specific aggregate conversion lift figures per individual antipattern fix (most evidence is bundled audit)
